Game Drama Score.details: Decided by 18 points with 8 lead changes and 17 ties. The game saw 0 major win probability swings, and the losing team's chances peaked at 53%.

Most Valuable Play.details: Q1, 5:55, White (BOS): White 26' 3PT Jump Shot (6 PTS) (Holiday 1 AST). It swung the win probability by +9.6 points.

Most Damaging Play.details: Q1, 2:21, Porziņģis (BOS): Porzingis 16' Jump Shot (8 PTS). It swung the win probability by -9.2 points.

Clutch-Time Scoring Summary.value: Scoring over the final five minutes: home team 7, away team 14.

recap.Biggest Turning Point: The game turned at Q1, 6:19: White 26' 3PT Jump Shot (3 PTS) (Tatum 3 AST). That single play moved BOS's chance of winning from 51% to 60%. No other moment shifted the game more, and it went BOS's way.
recap.Player Impact: No one influenced the outcome more than Porziņģis of BOS. Across 29 plays, his actions moved the win probability by a combined 30 percentage points, the most of any player on the floor.
recap.Comeback Reality: The tensest moment for a comeback came at Q1, 5:55, with DAL down 5 points. The model put their chance of pulling it off at 32%. In other words: possible, but not nothing.
recap.Hidden Momentum: The strongest run that didn't fully show on the scoreboard came at Q1, 7:56, with the game tilting BOS's way. The play in the middle of it: Tatum REBOUND (Off:1 Def:1).
recap.Model Note: These numbers come from ClutchCast's champion model, the winner of a competition between six models. The winner is judged on how honest its probabilities are on games it never trained on, not just how often it picks the right winner.
